title: Peer-review signoff record (Phase 4.5a)
description: Machine-readable sign-off entry recording that a second engineer has reviewed a compliance-gated pull request against the six-point rubric in docs/peer-review-guide.md.

$schema.description: Optional IDE-tooling hint pointing to this schema.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last time this file was updated (hand-editing is fine; the value is informational).

baseline_commit.description: Short or long SHA of the commit that introduced this file. Peer-review gating applies to commits AFTER this baseline; earlier content is grandfathered (see docs/peer-review-guide.md §6).

signoffs.description: One record per PR or direct commit that passed the peer-review gate. Append-only — never rewrite history.

pr.description: GitHub PR number with leading '#', or the literal string 'direct-commit' for landings that bypass a PR (discouraged).

date.description: ISO-8601 date (UTC) the sign-off was recorded.

commit.description: Short or long SHA of the commit being signed off.

author.description: GitHub handle (with or without leading '@') of the PR author. MUST differ from reviewer.

reviewer.description: GitHub handle (with or without leading '@') of the peer reviewer. MUST differ from author.

secondReviewer.description: Optional second reviewer for major refactors or 10+ UC PRs (see docs/peer-review-guide.md §5).

scope.description: UC IDs reviewed, plus meta-scope markers for non-UC paths (see enum pattern).

notes.description: Free-text reviewer note. REQUIRED when any check is 'fail' (explain the fix applied) or when 'derivatives' is 'n/a' (state why the UC is not derivative content). The author-differs-from-reviewer invariant is enforced by scripts/audit_peer_review_signoffs.py, not by this schema (JSON Schema cannot cross-compare sibling field values).

checkResult.description: pass = reviewer verified; fail = rubric point failed and was fixed before merge (note required); n/a = rubric point does not apply to this PR (note required only for 'derivatives').
